Instructions for serving Single-Page Applications with Flask by using static files and a catch-all endpoint.

# Purpose
The content explains how to use Flask to serve Single-Page Applications (SPA). It describes placing static files generated by a frontend framework in a subfolder within the project. Additionally, it outlines the need to create a catch-all endpoint to route all requests to the SPA. The example provided demonstrates setting up a Flask application with a static folder named `app` and a catch-all route that serves the `index.html` file for any path. The example also includes a `/heartbeat` endpoint that returns a JSON response indicating the application's health status.
